title: cistus umbellatus var. viscosus
intro: Cistus umbellatus var. viscosus, also known as Rockrose, is a flowering plant species in the Cistus genus, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cistus umbellatus var. viscosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Rockrose include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves, flowers, and roots of the plant.
